mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment rate above 5.5% signals weak labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and returns falls below -0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in inflation expectations creates a stagflationary environment that depresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to explain > 10% of return variance in out-of-sample tests, or returns contradict the predicted direction in 40%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with rising consumer confidence signals robust demand for goods and services, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: The joint effect of HOUST and RSAFS on returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) or the 60d forward returns are negative in 35%+ of cases with positive signals.

mechanism_narrative: A 2%+ year-over-year increase in CPI inflation triggers central bank tightening, raising discount rates and reducing equity valuations, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2%, or the predictive power of CPIAUCSL drops below 0.05 in rolling window tests.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in payroll employment and decline in unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to explain > 15% of return variance or the predicted direction is contradicted in 30%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate above 3% signals strong economic expansion, increasing corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 60d forward returns are negative in 40%+ of cases where GDPC1 exceeds 3% growth, or the correlation between GDPC1 and returns falls below 0.20.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production below 105.0 indicates weakening manufacturing activity, reducing corporate earnings and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below 105.0, or the predictive power of INDPRO drops below 0.07 in rolling window tests.
